codeReview.summary: Reviewed the prior failed review at `/home/shuv/repos/shuvdex/.factory/validation/cli/scrutiny/reviews/fix-cli-repo-paths.json`, the fix feature handoff and transcript skeleton for worker session `dfa94fb3-674b-42c8-aa33-178eee4fc9f7`, both relevant diffs (`966199114dc11feeeffcad1761f49e274f157a84` and `58f308f8e0c8717f7b473113d7eaf1df5f20b16e`), and the current CLI code/tests. The blocking runtime issue from the prior review is fixed in current code: `runSyncCommand()` now defaults `localRepoPath` to `process.cwd()` instead of `parsed.flags.repo`, so `fleet sync <skill> --repo <remote-path>` no longer validates against the remote path on the local machine. I found no remaining blocking defects in the fix itself. The only follow-up is test coverage: the added tests demonstrate flag separation and `runSync()` path independence, but they do not directly execute the `runSyncCommand()` / `run()` defaulting branch where the original bug lived.

issues.description: The new sync regression tests bypass the exact dispatcher branch that previously regressed. The runtime fix is in `apps/cli/src/cli.ts:573-576`, but `apps/cli/test/sync.test.ts:448-494` calls `runSync()` with an explicit `localRepoPath`, and `apps/cli/test/cli.test.ts:153-165` only checks that parsing leaves `localSkillPath` undefined. That means the added tests would not fail if `runSyncCommand()` were changed back to deriving its default local path from `--repo`, so the explicit regression scenario is not pinned end-to-end.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `cli-worker` skill still instructs workers to add CLI parsing with commander.js or yargs even though the repository's documented architecture and this fix both rely on the existing hand-rolled parser in `apps/cli/src/cli.ts`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/cli-worker/SKILL.md:62` says to add the command parser using commander.js or yargs, while `.factory/library/architecture.md:64` documents that the current CLI uses the hand-rolled parser in `apps/cli/src/cli.ts`. This fix also modifies that hand-rolled parser directly in `apps/cli/src/cli.ts:73-120,200,573-576`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `cli-worker` skill marks manual CLI verification as required, but this fix handoff recorded only test/typecheck/lint commands and still set `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` to true. That suggests the skill instructions and the procedure-feedback rubric are out of sync.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/cli-worker/SKILL.md:81-92` requires manual verification via `npm run build` and `./apps/cli/bin/fleet ...`, but the handoff at `/home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/handoffs/2026-03-15T23-22-23-239Z__fix-cli-sync-local-default__dfa94fb3-674b-42c8-aa33-178eee4fc9f7.json:14-28` lists only `npm test -- --filter @shuvdex/cli`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run lint`, while line 61 still records `"followedProcedure": true`.

summary: Pass. The current implementation fixes the prior blocking sync-path bug by making the default local repo path independent from `--repo` (`apps/cli/src/cli.ts:573-576`), so `fleet sync <skill> --repo <remote-path>` no longer reuses the remote path for local validation. I noted one non-blocking test-coverage gap: the added tests do not execute the dispatcher branch where the regression originally lived.
